title: How do I avoid investment fraud?
question_body: Community question about how do i avoid investment fraud?
answer_body: Check the seller's registration, be sceptical of guaranteed returns, and never let urgency rush a decision.

title: How do I avoid investment fraud?

title: How can I improve my credit score?
question_body: Community question about how can i improve my credit score?
answer_body: Pay on time, keep utilisation low, avoid unnecessary applications and check your reports for errors.

title: What should I do if I think an investment is a scam?
question_body: Community question about what should i do if i think an investment is a scam?
answer_body: Stop sending money, preserve all records, verify registration with the regulator, and report the suspected fraud.

title: What records do I need for investment taxes?
question_body: Community question about what records do i need for investment taxes?
answer_body: Keep trade confirmations, cost basis, dividend statements and broker 1099s; reconstruct basis before you sell.
combined_text: What records do I need for investment taxes? Community question about what records do i need for investment taxes? Keep trade confirmations, cost basis, dividend statements and broker 1099s; reconstruct basis before you sell. tax investing capital-gains

title: How do I start investing with little money?
question_body: Community question about how do i start investing with little money?
answer_body: Many brokers allow fractional shares, so you can begin with very small amounts.

title: Should I refinance my mortgage?
question_body: Community question about should i refinance my mortgage?
answer_body: Watch the term reset: lowering the rate but restarting 30 years can raise total interest paid.

title: How are capital gains taxed?
question_body: Community question about how are capital gains taxed?
answer_body: It depends on holding period, income, jurisdiction and cost basis. Separate short-term from long-term and keep records.

title: How much credit utilisation is healthy?
question_body: Community question about how much credit utilisation is healthy?
answer_body: Keeping reported utilisation under about 30%, and ideally under 10%, helps your score.
